skill.name: hig-components-controls
skill.description: Guides selection and input control choices using Apple Human Interface Guidelines for pickers, toggles, sliders, steppers, segmented controls, text inputs, labels, gauges, and related controls.
skill.summary: Choose Apple-style input and selection controls with state, validation, and accessibility guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found the high static risk score to be driven by Markdown documentation, not executable behavior. The external command, weak crypto, C2 keyword, reconnaissance, and hardcoded URL detections are false positives from inline Apple API names, image asset hashes, UI terminology, and documentation links. The only real concern is a low-impact instruction to check a local design context file before asking questions.

low_findings.title: Local Design Context File Read
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to check `.claude/apple-design-context.md` before asking questions. This is intended design-context lookup, but it may cause local project context to be read if the file exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and limited to one named context file. I found no evidence that it targets secrets or requests exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution False Positives
low_findings.description: Static external-command detections are inline Markdown references to files or Apple API names. They are not shell commands, script blocks, or executable code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose or Markdown links with inline code formatting. No command runner, shell invocation, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL False Positives
low_findings.description: Network detections are Apple Developer documentation and docs-assets links embedded in reference Markdown. The skill does not include code that fetches these URLs or sends data to them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to Apple documentation, Apple-hosted images, and the author site attribution. I found no network client code or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Blocker Pattern False Positives In UI Documentation
low_findings.description: Weak-crypto, C2 keyword, and reconnaissance detections come from hash-like image asset filenames and ordinary UI documentation terms such as control, device, keyboard, and locked screen. They do not indicate malware behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged strings are inside Apple HIG prose and image URLs. There is no cryptographic implementation, command-and-control logic, or system reconnaissance workflow.

content.value_statement: Choosing the wrong input control makes forms harder to use and less accessible. This skill applies Apple HIG guidance to recommend controls, state behavior, validation, and accessibility details.
actual_capabilities: Recommends controls for boolean, numeric, text, and choice-based input needs.
Compares toggles, segmented controls, pickers, sliders, steppers, combo boxes, and token fields.
Explains platform-specific guidance for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, watchOS, tvOS, and visionOS.
Provides state, validation, and accessibility considerations for control usage.
Uses local Apple HIG reference files included with the skill.
limitations: It does not generate production UI code by itself.
It covers selection and input controls, not every Apple HIG topic.
Apple guidance may change after the bundled reference files were captured.
It needs product context to make precise recommendations.

use_cases.title: Select Controls For A Settings Screen
use_cases.description: Choose toggles, checkboxes, sliders, or steppers based on state, range, and immediacy requirements.

use_cases.title: Review Form Interaction Patterns
use_cases.description: Check whether text fields, pickers, labels, and validation behavior match Apple platform expectations.

use_cases.title: Plan Cross-Platform Control Behavior
use_cases.description: Identify when macOS-only controls like combo boxes and token fields need alternatives on other platforms.

prompt_templates.title: Recommend A Control
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend the best Apple HIG control for this input: [describe the data type, option count, platform, and whether changes apply immediately]. Include why alternatives are less suitable.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this form against Apple HIG control guidance: [describe each field, available values, current controls, and target platforms]. Suggest control changes, validation timing, and labels.

prompt_templates.title: Map Platform Differences
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a platform control plan for [feature]. Cover iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and watchOS. Note unavailable controls and practical alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Define Control Specification
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a control specification for [workflow]. Include recommended controls, current-state display, commit behavior, validation rules, accessibility labels, and platform exceptions.

output_examples.input: A settings row lets users enable automatic backups on iPhone.
output_examples.output: Use a switch-style toggle in a list row. The row label provides context, and the change can apply immediately when the user turns it on or off.

output_examples.input: A macOS app lets users add multiple email recipients and reorder them.
output_examples.output: Use a token field because recipients become discrete editable values. Provide clear labels and support selection, deletion, and reordering behavior.

output_examples.input: A mobile form asks for a delivery date from many future dates.
output_examples.output: Use a date picker rather than segmented controls. It supports many values, familiar calendar interaction, and platform-standard date entry.

best_practices: Start with the input type, option count, platform, and commit behavior before choosing a control.
Use standard system controls unless a custom control solves a clear product need.
Specify labels, state display, validation timing, and VoiceOver behavior with every recommendation.
anti_patterns: Do not use segmented controls for long lists or unequal choices.
Do not use sliders when users need exact numeric input.
Do not rely on color alone to communicate selected or enabled state.

faq.question: Does this skill write UI code?
faq.answer: No. It provides control recommendations and design guidance that can inform implementation.

faq.question: Which Apple platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It references guidance for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, watchOS, tvOS, and visionOS where available.

faq.question: Can it choose between a picker and segmented control?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares option count, visual weight, platform, and interaction style.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for labels, state communication, hints, and VoiceOver considerations.

faq.question: Is the guidance always current?
faq.answer: No. It uses bundled reference files, so current Apple documentation may contain newer updates.

faq.question: Why does it check a local context file?
faq.answer: It checks for existing Apple design context so recommendations can match the current project.
